questions.q: Explain the concept of staffing and its significance in an organization. Discuss the factors that affect staffing and its impact on organizational performance.
questions.answer: Staffing refers to the process of selecting, training, and developing employees to meet the current and future needs of an organization. It is a crucial function of human resource management that plays a significant role in achieving organizational objectives. The factors that affect staffing include the size and structure of the organization, the type of job, the level of technology, and the availability of skilled labor. Effective staffing can lead to increased productivity, improved morale, and better job satisfaction, ultimately resulting in enhanced organizational performance.
marking_scheme: Explain the concept of staffing (1 mark)
Discuss the significance of staffing in an organization (1 mark)
List the factors that affect staffing (1 mark)
Describe the impact of staffing on organizational performance (2 marks)

questions.q: Distinguish between internal and external sources of recruitment. Explain the advantages and disadvantages of each source.
questions.answer: Internal sources of recruitment refer to the process of filling job vacancies from within the organization, while external sources refer to the process of recruiting candidates from outside the organization. The advantages of internal sources include reduced recruitment costs, faster time-to-hire, and improved employee retention. On the other hand, the disadvantages include limited pool of candidates and potential biases. The advantages of external sources include access to a wider pool of candidates, better skills and qualifications, and improved diversity. However, the disadvantages include higher recruitment costs, longer time-to-hire, and potential integration challenges.
marking_scheme: Distinguish between internal and external sources of recruitment (1 mark)
Explain the advantages of internal sources of recruitment (1 mark)
Explain the disadvantages of internal sources of recruitment (1 mark)
Explain the advantages of external sources of recruitment (1 mark)
Explain the disadvantages of external sources of recruitment (1 mark)

questions.q: A company is planning to recruit 100 new employees. If the recruitment cost per employee is ₹5,000, calculate the total recruitment cost.
questions.answer:
Total recruitment cost = Number of employees x Recruitment cost per employee
= 100 x ₹5,000
= ₹5,00,000
marking_scheme: Calculate the total recruitment cost (5 marks)

questions.q: A company has an employee turnover rate of 20%. If the company has 500 employees, calculate the number of employees who left the company in the past year.
questions.answer:
Number of employees who left = Employee turnover rate x Total number of employees
= 20% x 500
= 0.20 x 500
= 100
marking_scheme: Calculate the number of employees who left the company (5 marks)

questions.q: Describe the role of training and development in staffing. Explain the benefits of training and development for employees and the organization.
questions.answer: Training and development play a crucial role in staffing by enhancing the skills and knowledge of employees to meet the changing needs of the organization. The benefits of training and development for employees include improved job satisfaction, increased confidence, and better career prospects. For the organization, the benefits include improved productivity, enhanced competitiveness, and better employee retention. Training and development can be provided through various methods, including on-the-job training, classroom training, and online courses.
marking_scheme: Describe the role of training and development in staffing (1 mark)
Explain the benefits of training and development for employees (1 mark)
Explain the benefits of training and development for the organization (1 mark)
List the methods of training and development (1 mark)
Discuss the impact of training and development on organizational performance (1 mark)

questions.q: A company is planning to introduce a new product line. If the company requires 50 new employees to produce the product, and the recruitment cost per employee is ₹10,000, calculate the total recruitment cost for the new product line.
questions.answer:
Total recruitment cost = Number of employees x Recruitment cost per employee
= 50 x ₹10,000
= ₹5,00,000
marking_scheme: Calculate the total recruitment cost (5 marks)
